description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, dark wooden handle with a pronounced curve, designed for ergonomic grip and efficient swinging. The handle appears to be made from a single piece of wood, showing a natural grain and a slightly rough, matte finish. The axe head is forged from dark metal, with a broad, rectangular blade and a thick poll, giving it a utilitarian and heavy-duty appearance. The blade edge is straight and wide, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks, and the head is securely mounted onto the handle in a traditional style. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing its practical, workmanlike design. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, making it suitable for use in urban maintenance, gardening, or emergency scenarios.
description: A sturdy, curved wooden-handled axe with a broad, dark metal blade and a utilitarian, practical design. The axe is robust and functional, with no visible branding or decorative features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rectangular metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thickness of the axe head and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the straight edge of the blade and the handle's grain.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the poll of the axe head and the end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and matte finish.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, rectangular steel blade with a straight edge and thick poll, single cutting edge, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
